brief_document.title: aion_kit/json: project, merge, and pluck over JSON
brief_document.purpose: Delivers the data transformation/wrangling module: project (decode a thin typed view, ignoring the rest — the ADR-012 facts projection for plain JSON), merge (append-only deep merge, order-stable — the enrichment pattern generalised from enrich.gleam's merge helpers), and pluck (path get). This is how a family wrangles report JSON without re-deriving the merge and projection logic brief_dev open-coded (S4). Pure and deterministic (CN2): safe on either side of the determinism boundary (S7). Depends on KIT-001 only for the package skeleton; it shares no files with KIT-002 and is parallel-runnable with it once KIT-001 lands.
brief_document.task: Create gleam/aion_kit/src/aion_kit/json.gleam and gleam/aion_kit/test/json_test.gleam. project(input, decoder): decode a thin typed view from a JSON string with a caller-supplied decoder, succeeding even when the JSON carries fields the view does not name (the unnamed fields are dropped, not an error) — the project sibling of payload.peek, for plain JSON strings rather than a Sealed. merge(left, right): deep-merge two JSON values append-only and order-stable — objects merge key-by-key with the right value winning a leaf conflict, arrays concatenate left-then-right, and existing key insertion order is preserved (the generalisation of enrich.gleam's append-only stage merges). pluck(value, path): read the value at a path (a list of object keys) and return Ok(value) or a typed miss when any key is absent or a non-object is traversed — never a panic, never a guessed default (ADR-001). Use gleam/json and gleam/dynamic/decode (the conventions aion_flow/codec uses); model the typed miss with a small result/option type, returning codec.DecodeError-style typed failures where a decode is involved. In scope: the json module, its tests including the negative cases (C16), and the purity guarantee for the file this brief adds (re-verifying C17/C18's properties for json.gleam, which KIT-001 owns as cluster checklist items — split noted). Out of scope: payload (KIT-001), template (KIT-002), schema validation or codegen (design non-goal), and any reshape of enrich.gleam (RM-023).

requirements.title: project: a thin typed view that ignores the rest
requirements.spec: In gleam/aion_kit/src/aion_kit/json.gleam, define project. WHEN project(input, decoder) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL decode the thin typed view named by the caller-supplied decoder from the JSON string input and SHALL succeed when the input carries additional fields the decoder does not name (those fields are ignored, not an error). IF the input is not valid JSON, or lacks a field the decoder requires, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return a typed codec.DecodeError and SHALL NOT panic. project SHALL NOT return any field the decoder does not name, and SHALL be pure (no IO, no clock, no entropy).
acceptance: For input "{\"a\":1,\"b\":2,\"c\":3}" and a decoder naming only field a, project returns Ok of a view carrying a == 1, with b and c absent from the result.
project("not json", decoder) returns Error(codec.DecodeError(...)) and does not panic.
project of a JSON object lacking a field the decoder requires returns Error(codec.DecodeError(...)).
grep -E 'panic|let assert' gleam/aion_kit/src/aion_kit/json.gleam produces no matches.
cd gleam/aion_kit && gleam test runs json_test and the project cases (including the extra-field case) pass.

requirements.title: merge: append-only deep merge, order-stable
requirements.spec: Add merge to gleam/aion_kit/src/aion_kit/json.gleam. WHEN merge(left, right) is called on two JSON values, THE SYSTEM SHALL deep-merge them: WHEN both are objects, THE SYSTEM SHALL merge key-by-key, recursing into shared keys, taking right-only keys, keeping left-only keys, and preserving the existing left-key insertion order with right-only keys appended in their right-side order; WHEN both are arrays, THE SYSTEM SHALL concatenate left then right; in every other case (a leaf conflict or a type mismatch) THE SYSTEM SHALL take the right value (right wins). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT mutate, sort, or deduplicate; the merge is append-only and order-stable. THE SYSTEM SHALL be pure (no IO, no clock, no entropy).
acceptance: merge of {"a":1,"b":{"x":1}} and {"b":{"y":2},"c":3} equals {"a":1,"b":{"x":1,"y":2},"c":3} with keys in order a, b, c and b's keys in order x, y.
merge of {"v":1} and {"v":2} equals {"v":2} (right wins a leaf conflict).
merge of {"xs":[1,2]} and {"xs":[3]} equals {"xs":[1,2,3]} (arrays concatenate left-then-right).
merge of {"k":1} and {"k":[9]} equals {"k":[9]} (type-mismatch leaf takes the right value).
cd gleam/aion_kit && gleam test runs json_test and the merge cases pass.

requirements.title: pluck: path get with a typed miss
requirements.spec: Add pluck to gleam/aion_kit/src/aion_kit/json.gleam. WHEN pluck(value, path) is called with path a list of object keys, THE SYSTEM SHALL walk the keys in order into nested objects and return Ok of the value found at the end of the path. IF any key in the path is absent, or a non-object value is encountered before the path is exhausted, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return the typed miss and SHALL NOT panic and SHALL NOT substitute a guessed or default value (ADR-001). pluck with the empty path SHALL return Ok of the input value unchanged. pluck SHALL be pure (no IO, no clock, no entropy).
acceptance: pluck of {"a":{"b":{"c":7}}} with path ["a","b","c"] returns Ok of the JSON value 7.
pluck of {"a":{"b":1}} with path ["a","x"] returns the typed miss (key x absent).
pluck of {"a":1} with path ["a","b"] returns the typed miss (a is a non-object, cannot traverse into b).
pluck of any value with the empty path [] returns Ok of that same value.
cd gleam/aion_kit && gleam test runs json_test and the pluck cases pass.

requirements.title: Negative-case coverage for project, merge, and pluck
requirements.spec: In gleam/aion_kit/test/json_test.gleam, cover the negative case of each function so a regression is caught (S9). THE SYSTEM SHALL include a test where project decodes a view from JSON carrying fields the view does not name (the extra fields are dropped, decode succeeds); a test where merge resolves a leaf conflict right-wins; and a test where pluck returns the typed miss for a missing path. THE SYSTEM SHALL assert each negative case returns the documented typed result (Ok-with-ignored-extras, right value, typed miss) rather than an error, a panic, or a guessed default. This requirement delivers C16 and re-verifies C18's purity for json.gleam (C18 owned by KIT-001 — split noted in this brief's task and KIT-001's).
acceptance: The json_test module contains a project test asserting an extra-field JSON decodes to the thin view (the ignored fields are absent, the decode is Ok).
The json_test module contains a merge test asserting a leaf conflict resolves to the right value.
The json_test module contains a pluck test asserting a missing path returns the typed miss (asserting the miss branch explicitly).
grep -E 'panic|let assert' gleam/aion_kit/src/aion_kit/json.gleam produces no matches in library code.
cd gleam/aion_kit && gleam test runs json_test and all negative-case tests pass.

boundaries: SHALL NOT create or modify aion_kit/payload.gleam or aion_kit/template.gleam — those are KIT-001 and KIT-002.
SHALL NOT import or modify examples/stacked-dev/src/stacked_dev/enrich.gleam — json.merge is the standalone generalisation; reshaping enrich.gleam to consume it is RM-023.
SHALL NOT add a JSON schema validator, schema codegen, or any $ref/$defs/oneOf/anyOf/default handling — json is value-level wrangling, not schema validation (ADR-007, design non-goal).
SHALL NOT add a merge policy argument, a depth/size cap, or a default for a missing pluck path — absent input is a typed result, never a guessed default (ADR-001).
SHALL NOT import aion/workflow, aion/activity, aion/query, aion/signal, or perform any IO (CN2).
verification: cd gleam/aion_kit && gleam build && gleam test && gleam format --check all exit 0 (json suite runs).
grep -E 'import aion/(workflow|activity|query|signal)' gleam/aion_kit/src/aion_kit/json.gleam produces no matches.
grep -E 'panic|let assert' gleam/aion_kit/src/aion_kit/json.gleam produces no matches in library code (re-verifying C18's purity for the file this brief adds; C18 is owned by KIT-001).
grep -F 'enrich' gleam/aion_kit/src/aion_kit/json.gleam produces no matches (json imports nothing from the example).
gleam/aion_kit/src/aion_kit/json.gleam is at or under 500 code lines (excluding comments and whitespace).

adrs.title: No arbitrary limits, no assumed defaults
adrs.decision: Configurable values come from the builder/author or are deferred to the layer that owns them (e.g. beamr's own defaults). No caps, rate limits, or hardcoded defaults invented at the aion layer. Values are discussed before implementation. Rejected: convenience defaults — they are decisions made for the user without telling them.

adrs.title: No backwards compatibility during the build
adrs.decision: Replace, don't add alongside. No compat shims, no zombie code, no #[deprecated] markers. Breaking changes are made cleanly and consumers move forward. Rejected: incremental deprecation cycles — they double the surface under test for an audience of zero.

adrs.title: Workflow code stays thin: large activity results ride as opaque payloads
adrs.decision: Workflow code stays thin. Large activity results ride between stages as opaque sealed payloads the workflow never opens; the workflow decodes only a small facts projection — the few fields it needs to route control flow (pass/fail, changed files, blocked, drift). Decoding and prompt rendering happen in the consuming activity on the worker, which has a full heap and runs the work once rather than on every replay. Rejected: decoding and rendering full reports in workflow code — it puts the determinism boundary's heavy lifting in the wrong place and was the regression that exposed the beamr crash.
adrs.quote: we're not changing the output of the agents right we're still keeping all of that which is not decoding it all within the workflow so it also stays packaged up and then it passes out to the worker the worker unpacks it and provides the prompt to the agent

checklist.text: aion_kit/json.project decodes a thin typed view from a JSON string using a caller-supplied decoder and succeeds even when the JSON carries fields the view does not name (the ignored fields are dropped, not an error).

aion_kit/json.merge deep-merges two JSON values append-only and order-stable: objects merge key-by-key with the right value winning a leaf conflict, arrays concatenate left-then-right, and existing key insertion order is preserved.

aion_kit/json.pluck reads the value at a path (a list of object keys) from a JSON value and returns it, or a typed miss when any key in the path is absent or a non-object is traversed — never a panic and never a guessed default.

Each of project, merge, and pluck has a negative-case test: a field absent from the projection still decodes, a leaf conflict in merge resolves right-wins, and a missing pluck path returns the typed miss.

stories.text: As an activity author, I want to deep-merge a stage report into a document append-only and order-stable so that enrichment is a library call with right-wins leaf semantics rather than a bespoke per-family merge.

As a workflow author, I want every toolkit primitive to be pure and deterministic so that I can call the same projection and JSON helpers from workflow code or activity code without risking a replay divergence.

As a future maintainer, I want every primitive's negative cases (an absent projection field, a merge leaf conflict, a missing path, an unmatched placeholder) covered by tests with typed results so that I can change an implementation and trust the suite to catch a regression.

constraints.text: Runtime-agnostic: no module in aion_kit or the aion_flow payload module imports or references the norn agent driver, any agent/process-spawning runtime, or engine-runtime internals; grep for 'norn' and for process/port spawning in the package finds nothing (ADR-011).

Pure and deterministic: every public function in aion_kit/template, aion_kit/json, aion_kit/payload, and every aion_flow Sealed accessor reads no wall clock, draws no entropy, and performs no IO — they are total functions of their arguments and safe to call from workflow code or activity code (invariant 2).

Hold-without-decode: the Sealed type exposes no API that returns a decoded structured view of its contents; the only reads are raw (the opaque bytes) and peek (a caller-supplied thin decoder). Holding a Sealed on the workflow heap costs one binary, never a decoded nested structure (ADR-012).

No imported wire schemas and no $ref/$defs/oneOf/anyOf/default in anything this cluster introduces; aion_kit ships no JSON schema files, and the json module is value-level wrangling, not schema validation (ADR-007).

No arbitrary defaults or limits: no module supplies a default content-type, a default merge policy, a size or depth cap, or any hardcoded bound; absent input (a missing projection field, a missing pluck path, a decode failure) is returned as a typed result the caller handles (ADR-001).

No backwards-compatibility shims: aion_kit does not re-export, wrap, or alias brief_dev's bespoke prompts.gleam/enrich.gleam helpers or aion_client's Payload; it is the replacement those call sites move to under RM-023, not a parallel path (ADR-002).

Each source file stays at or under 500 code lines (excluding tests, comments, whitespace); no #[allow]-equivalent suppression, and no Gleam panic/assert/let-assert in library code — decode and lookup failures are returned as Result/Option values.

resolved_context.intention: Authoring a new aion workflow family should be writing prompts, schemas, and a gate — not re-deriving the data plumbing underneath every one. When this lands, the plumbing that brief_dev hand-rolled inside one example (text composition for prompts, JSON wrangling for reports and enrichment, and a way to carry an activity result a workflow never wants to open) is a small, well-tested package that ships with the worker, so the next family inherits all of it for free. aion_kit is a toolbox of cross-cutting primitives, deliberately lean: it carries only what genuinely applies across the board, and it stays out of the determinism boundary's way by being pure. The norn agent driver and every other runtime harness stay where they belong — in the worker layer that consumers own (ADR-011) — so the standard library couples to no particular agent runtime and ships no consumer-specific concern.
